Minutes of the meeting of the Committee constituted in the Legislative Department for
identifying suitability of posts for various categories of disabilities covered under the
Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 held on 18.03.2025 at 11:00 AM in the
Department
A follow up meeting of the Committee constituted for identifying suitability of posts in the
Legislative Department for the categories of disabilities mentioned in THE RIGHTS OF
PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES ACT, 2016 was held on 18.03.2025 at 11:00 AM in the
chamber of Additional Secretary (Admn.), Legislative Department, Room No. 421, 'A' Wing,
4th Floor, Shastri Bhawan, New Delhi. Composition of the Committee is as under:
1. Shri Udaya Kumara, Additional Secretary (Admn.)
- Chairman
2. Shri Diwakar Singh, Additional Secretary (VSP)
- Member
3. Dr. Brajesh Kumar, Joint Secretary & Legislative Counsel - Member
(OL Wing)
4. Shri Hans Raj Meena, Deputy Secretary (Admn.)
- Member
5. Shri Prashant Singh, Deputy Legislative Counsel
- Member (PwBD)
6. Shri Rajesh Srivastava, Assistant Legislative Counsel
- Member (PwBD)
(Hindi) (OL Wing)
7. Dr. N. Visakamurthy, Deputy Government Advocate
- Member (PwBD)
8. Shri Pramod Kumar Gupta, President,
- Member (PwBD)
All India Association for Differently Abled (AIADA)
2. The Legislative Department is mainly concerned with drafting of all principal legislation
and sub-ordinate legislation for the Central Government. It is also concerned with election Laws
and with certain matters relating to List III of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution like
personal law, contracts evidence etc. Official Languages Wing of the Legislative Department is
responsible for preparing and publishing standard legal terminology and also for translating into
Hindi, all the Bills to be introduced in Parliament, all Central Acts, Ordinances, Subordinate
legislations, etc., as required under the Official Languages Act, 1963. This Wing is also
responsible for arranging translation of the Central Acts, Ordinances, etc., into the Official
Languages as specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution. Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan is
mainly concerned with bringing out authoritative Hindi versions of reportable judgments of the
Supreme Court and the High Courts with the objective of promoting the progressive use of Hindi
in the legal field. Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan brings out various publications of legal literature in
Hindi.
3. The Committee was informed that as per Section 34 of the RPWD Act, 2016, 4%
reservation is to be extended in each group of posts i.e. A, B and C to persons with benchmark
disabilities. The details of the categories of PwDs and their reservation percentage are as under:
1%
Category of Persons with Disabilities
Reservation
Percentage
(a) blindness and low vision
1%
(b) deaf and hard of hearing
(c) locomotor disability including cerebral palsy, leprosy cured, dwarfism, 1%
acid attack victims and muscular dystrophy
(d) autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability and mental 1%
illness
(e) multiple disabilities from amongst persons under clauses (a) to (d)
including deaf-blindness
Continue...

4. During the meeting, Dr. Brajesh Kumar, JS&LC (OL Wing) informed that the posts of
JS&LC (Hindi Branch) and Addl. LC (Hindi Branch) are no longer filled through direct
recruitment. The list of the posts in the Legislative Department including Vidhi Sahitya
Prakashan and Official Languages Wing for which direct recruitment is one of the mode of
filling up the vacancies is as under :-

5. The Committee deliberated on the physical requirement and functional classification of
the above said posts taking into account Department of Empowerment of Persons with
Disabilities (DEPWD) Notification No. 38-16/2020-DD-III dated 04.01.2021.
6. The need to follow the principal list of posts as identified by the DEPWD was discussed
during the meeting. It was stated that reservation in employment as per the RPwD Act, 2016 is
crucial to promote inclusivity and ensuring equal opportunities for the persons with benchmark
disabilities. Shri Pramod Kumar Gupta stated that special emphasis needs to be given to persons
with intellectual disability and mental illness to empower them and bring them into the
mainstream. Further, with the latest advancement in assistive technologies and AI tools, PwDs
can now do things which were not possible a few years ago. Accordingly, a liberal view may be
taken and no category of PwBD may be restricted from accessing the reservation benefits in
employment as per the RPWD Act, 2016.
7. The committee also emphasized on the need to maintain proper reservation roster for
PwDs to ensure that the reservation policy as per the guidelines issued by DOPT and DEPWD is
being followed.

T

8. While concluding, the Committee unanimously decided that the principal list of posts with
corresponding functional requirements and suitable category of Benchmark Disabilities for each post as
identified by the DEPWD may be followed for the posts mentioned in para 4 above.

messages.content:
This document contains the minutes of a meeting held on March 18, 2025, in the Legislative Department.

**Purpose of the meeting:**
The Committee met to identify which posts in the Legislative Department are suitable for various categories of disabilities, as covered by the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 (RPWD Act, 2016).

**Key points discussed:**
*   The Legislative Department's main roles include drafting laws and handling election laws.
*   As per Section 34 of the RPWD Act, 2016, 4% reservation is required in each group of posts (A, B, C) for persons with benchmark disabilities, with 1% specifically for categories like blindness, deafness, locomotor disability, autism, and multiple disabilities.
*   A list of posts in the Legislative Department that are filled through direct recruitment was presented.
*   The Committee discussed the physical requirements and functional classification of these posts, referring to a notification from the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPWD).
*   It was emphasized that reservation in employment is crucial for inclusivity and equal opportunities.
*   Shri Pramod Kumar Gupta suggested a liberal view, considering advancements in assistive technologies, so that no category of persons with benchmark disabilities is restricted from reservation benefits.
*   The need to maintain a proper reservation roster for persons with disabilities was also highlighted.

**Decision:**
The Committee unanimously decided to follow the principal list of posts, including their functional requirements and suitable categories of Benchmark Disabilities, as identified by the DEPWD, for the posts listed in the document.

The minutes were later approved by Harsh Gupta on April 2, 2025.

messages.content:
This document contains the minutes of a meeting held on March 18, 2025, at 11:00 AM in the Legislative Department.

**Purpose of the Meeting:**
The Committee was constituted to identify suitable posts for various categories of disabilities covered under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 (RPwD Act, 2016) within the Legislative Department.

**Key Information and Discussions:**
*   The Legislative Department's main functions include drafting legislation, election laws, and matters related to List III of the Seventh Schedule. Its Official Languages Wing handles legal terminology and translations, and Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan promotes Hindi in the legal field.
*   As per Section 34 of the RPwD Act, 2016, 4% reservation is required in each group of posts (A, B, C) for persons with benchmark disabilities, with 1% allocated to specific disability categories (blindness/low vision, deaf/hard of hearing, locomotor disability, autism/intellectual disability/specific learning disability/mental illness, and multiple disabilities).
*   A list of posts in the Legislative Department, Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan, and Official Languages Wing, for which direct recruitment is a method of filling vacancies, was presented.
*   The Committee discussed the physical requirements and functional classification of these posts, considering a notification from the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPWD).
*   It was emphasized that reservation is crucial for inclusivity and equal opportunities. Shri Pramod Kumar Gupta suggested a liberal view, considering advancements in assistive technologies, so that no category of persons with benchmark disabilities is restricted from reservation benefits.
*   The need to maintain a proper reservation roster for persons with disabilities was also highlighted.

**Decision:**
The Committee unanimously decided to follow the principal list of posts, including their functional requirements and suitable categories of Benchmark Disabilities, as identified by the DEPWD, for the posts discussed.

The minutes were approved by Harsh Gupta on April 2, 2025.

messages.content:
This document contains the minutes of a meeting held on March 18, 2025, at 11:00 AM in New Delhi. The meeting was conducted by a Committee in the Legislative Department to identify suitable job posts for persons with various disabilities, as required by the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016.

The Committee was chaired by Shri Udaya Kumara and included seven other members, including representatives from the Legislative Department and the President of the All India Association for Differently Abled.

Key points discussed:
*   The Legislative Department's main functions, including drafting laws and translating legal texts.
*   Section 34 of the RPwD Act, 2016, which mandates a 4% reservation in posts (Groups A, B, and C) for persons with benchmark disabilities, with 1% for each of five specific disability categories.
*   A list of 34 posts in the Legislative Department (including its Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan and Official Languages Wing) that are filled through direct recruitment was presented.
*   The Committee discussed the physical requirements and functional classification for these posts, considering a notification from the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPWD).
*   It was suggested that a liberal view should be taken regarding reservation benefits due to advancements in assistive technologies and AI, especially for persons with intellectual disability and mental illness.
*   The need to maintain proper reservation rosters for persons with disabilities was also emphasized.

The Committee unanimously decided to follow the principal list of posts, functional requirements, and suitable categories of benchmark disabilities as identified by the DEPWD for the 34 posts mentioned in the document. The minutes were later approved by Harsh Gupta on April 2, 2025.

during the meeting. It was stated that reservation in employment as per the RPwD Act, 2016 is
crucial to promote inclusivity and ensuring equal opportunities for the persons with benchmark
disabilities. Shri Pramod Kumar Gupta stated that special emphasis needs to be given to persons
with intellectual disability and mental illness to empower them and bring them into the
mainstream. Further, with the latest advancement in assistive technologies and AI tools, PwDs
can now do things which were not possible a few years ago. Accordingly, a liberal view may be
taken and no category of PwBD may be restricted from accessing the reservation benefits in
employment as per the RPWD Act, 2016.
7. The committee also emphasized on the need to maintain proper reservation roster for
PwDs to ensure that the reservation policy as per the guidelines issued by DOPT and DEPWD is
being followed.

sources.document_text: Minutes of the meeting of the Committee constituted in the Legislative Department for identifying suitability of posts for various categories of disabilities covered under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 held on 18.03.2025 at 11:00 AM in the Department A follow up meeting of the Committee constituted for identifying suitability of posts in the Legislative Department for the categories of disabilities mentioned in THE RIGHTS OF PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES ACT, 2016 was held on 18.03.2025 at 11:00 AM in the chamber of Additional Secretary (Admn.), Legislative Department, Room No. 421, 'A' Wing, 4th Floor, Shastri Bhawan, New Delhi. Composition of the Committee is as under: 1. Shri Udaya Kumara, Additional Secretary (Admn.) - Chairman 2. Shri Diwakar Singh, Additional Secretary (VSP) - Member 3. Dr. Brajesh Kumar, Joint Secretary & Legislative Counsel - Member (OL Wing) 4. Shri Hans Raj Meena, Deputy Secretary (Admn.) - Member 5. Shri Prashant Singh, Deputy Legislative Counsel - Member (PwBD) 6. Shri Rajesh Srivastava, Assistant Legislative Counsel - Member (PwBD) (Hindi) (OL Wing) 7. Dr. N. Visakamurthy, Deputy Government Advocate - Member (PwBD) 8. Shri Pramod Kumar Gupta, President, - Member (PwBD) All India Association for Differently Abled (AIADA) 2. The Legislative Department is mainly concerned with drafting of all principal legislation and sub-ordinate legislation for the Central Government. It is also concerned with election Laws and with certain matters relating to List III of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution like personal law, contracts evidence etc. Official Languages Wing of the Legislative Department is responsible for preparing and publishing standard legal terminology and also for translating into Hindi, all the Bills to be introduced in Parliament, all Central Acts, Ordinances, Subordinate legislations, etc., as required under the Official Languages Act, 1963. This Wing is also responsible for arranging translation of the Central Acts, Ordinances, etc., into the Official Languages as specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution. Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan is mainly concerned with bringing out authoritative Hindi versions of reportable judgments of the Supreme Court and the High Courts with the objective of promoting the progressive use of Hindi in the legal field. Vidhi Sahitya Prakashan brings out various publications of legal literature in Hindi. 3. The Committee was informed that as per Section 34 of the RPWD Act, 2016, 4% reservation is to be extended in each group of posts i.e. A, B and C to persons with benchmark disabilities. The details of the categories of PwDs and their reservation percentage are as under: 1% Category of Persons with Disabilities Reservation Percentage (a) blindness and low vision 1% (b) deaf and hard of hearing (c) locomotor disability including cerebral palsy, leprosy cured, dwarfism, 1% acid attack victims and muscular dystrophy (d) autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability and mental 1% illness (e) multiple disabilities from amongst persons under clauses (a) to (d) including deaf-blindness Continue... -2- 4.
